# Olympic Inspections & Testing — Mold & Air Quality Testing on the Olympic Peninsula

> Olympic Inspections & Testing is a family-owned mold inspection and indoor air quality testing service for residential, commercial, automotive, marine, and RV environments across the Olympic Peninsula in Washington State. Established 2025. ISO/IEC 17025-accredited laboratory partner. Tests for over 100 types of mold and other particles. Independent — we do not perform remediation, so the inspection is honest. Inspections start at $150.

## About

Family-owned and operated. Established 2025 to fill a critical gap in high-quality air and mold testing services on the Olympic Peninsula. The business was founded after the owners discovered mold in their newly built home — an experience that turned into a frustrating and expensive ordeal because the Peninsula had few service providers, sky-high prices, and unanswered calls. They set out to offer reliable, affordable, and straightforward air and mold testing.

## Services

- **Complete Mold Inspection** — non-invasive walkthrough using thermal imaging to detect hidden moisture, collect humidity data, and identify areas of concern. Comprehensive report with findings and sampling recommendations.
- **DIY Microbial Air & Surface Sampling** — sampling-only service for clients who already know what they need, or contractors verifying that air and surfaces are clear.
- **ERMI Testing** — for clients working with healthcare professionals who need to document or track a history of mold exposure. Affordable and accessible.
- **Written Lab Reports** — photo-documented, plain-English, formatted for insurance, real estate, or remediation contractors. Each report checks for over 100 types of mold and other particles.

## Markets We Serve

- **Residential** — homeowners, renters, landlords, property managers
- **Commercial** — offices, retail, restaurants, commercial buildings
- **Automotive** — personal vehicles, work trucks, vans, fleet cars
- **Marine** — sailboats, yachts, fishing boats, houseboats
- **RV & Trailer** — motorhomes, travel trailers, campers, fifth wheels

## Pricing

- Full mold inspections start at just **$150** — the most affordable, certified, quality-driven option on the Peninsula
- Air sample add-on: $85 each
- Surface or swab sample: $55 each
- Priority 24-hour report: +$150
- Thermal/infrared scan: +$95
- Final price confirmed after the on-site walkthrough

## Process

1. Book online or call — same-day reply
2. On-site inspection — 1 to 2 hours
3. Lab analysis at our ISO/IEC 17025-accredited lab — 2 to 3 days
4. Written report delivered — fast turnaround

## What the Lab Tests For

The lab analyzes for over 100 types of mold and other particles, including:

- Stachybotrys (Black Mold)
- Cladosporium
- Chaetomium
- Ulocladium
- Aspergillus / Penicillium
- Pollen
- Insect parts
- Human and animal skin cells
- Dust and other small particles

## Service Area

Olympic Peninsula, Washington State. Specifically: Sequim, Port Angeles, Port Townsend, Forks, Hadlock, Chimacum, and Quilcene. Travel fees may apply for very rural or remote properties — confirmed in writing before booking.

## Contact

- Phone: (360) 670-3367
- Email: hello@olympicinspections.com
- Address: Sequim, WA 98382
- Booking: https://bluejayportfolio.com/sites/olympic-inspections/#book
- Pricing estimator: https://bluejayportfolio.com/sites/olympic-inspections/#calculator

## Why Choose Olympic Inspections & Testing

- Family-owned, founded 2025 by a Peninsula family who discovered mold in their own home and decided to do it better
- Independent — we don't perform remediation, so the inspection is honest. Zero financial incentive to find more than is there.
- ISO/IEC 17025-accredited laboratory partner
- No upselling, ever
- Affordable base pricing — full mold inspections start at $150 (most local competitors charge $1,500–$2,500)
- Real-estate ready — reports formatted for buyer/seller use, routinely shared with realtors, escrow, and insurance adjusters
- Local — we live on the Peninsula, we know the marine air, the older homes, the well-water issues
